Class LifecycleManager

java.lang.Object
simcli.engine.LifecycleManager
public class LifecycleManager extends Object
LifecycleManager — centralizes all daily aging, life-stage transitions, death-by-old-age, and retirement logic for every Sim in the neighborhood.

Responsibility (Single Responsibility Principle)

This class does exactly one thing: process day boundaries for each Sim's lifecycle. It increments daysAlive, triggers Sim.ageUp() at the correct interval, handles death-by-old-age, pension collection, and forced retirement. It knows nothing about needs, inventory, rendering, or input — those concerns belong elsewhere.

How it fits into the State Pattern

processDay(Sim) delegates to Sim.ageUp(), which in turn calls currentStage.getNextStage(age) on the Sim's private stage. If the stage returns a new object, the Sim swaps its "brain" polymorphically. This manager never touches the stage directly — it only tells the Sim that a birthday has occurred.

Encapsulation

All lifecycle logic that was previously scattered inside Sim and GameLoop is now consolidated here, making it easier to test, modify, and reason about aging rules in isolation.

  • Constructor Details

    • LifecycleManager

      public LifecycleManager(int daysPerAgeTick, SimulationLogger logger)
      Creates a new LifecycleManager.
      Parameters:
      daysPerAgeTick - number of in-game days that constitute one age-up cycle. Must be >= 1.
      logger - the simulation logger for lifecycle messages.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if daysPerAgeTick is less than 1.

  • Method Details

    • processDayForAll

      public void processDayForAll(List<Sim> neighborhood)
      Processes a day boundary for every Sim in the neighborhood.

      This is the main entry point called by GameLoop whenever a new day begins. It iterates over all Sims and delegates individual processing to processDay(Sim). Dead Sims are skipped.

      Parameters:
      neighborhood - the list of all Sims in the game world.

    • processDay

      public void processDay(Sim sim)
      Processes a single day boundary for one Sim.

      Increments the Sim's day counter via Sim.incrementDaysAlive(), resets daily career counters, and checks whether the Sim has reached an age-up boundary (every daysPerAgeTick days). On an age-up:

      • Calls Sim.ageUp() to increment age and trigger the State Pattern transition if applicable.
      • Checks if the Sim has reached GameConstants.DEATH_AGE and marks them as dead.
      • Awards retirement pension to unemployed elders.
      • Forces retirement if the Sim exceeds their job's max age.
      Parameters:
      sim - the Sim to process; must not be null.
      Throws:
      IllegalArgumentException - if sim is null.

    • getDaysPerAgeTick

      public int getDaysPerAgeTick()
      Returns the configured number of days per age-up cycle.
      Returns:
      days-per-age-tick value set at construction.
